reasoning: FIDE rating lists are updated on the 1st of every month. Thus, on 2025-04-20, Wikipedia will still reflect Alina Kashlinskaya's April 1st rating of 2496. A 1% increase requires her to reach a rating of 2521 (2496 * 1.01 = 2520.96) on the Wikipedia page. For April 20, this is virtually impossible, so the probability is strictly the minimum allowed (set to 0.05). In May, she will have completed the FIDE Women's Grand Prix in India (April 13-24). Gaining ~25 points in a single top tier 9-11 round event against equal or slightly weaker opposition requires a truly exceptional performance heavily exceeding expectations (scoring perhaps +6 or +7 in 9 games), making it highly improbable but non-zero, hence 0.06. Over longer horizons (July, October), she will compete in more events, leading to a broader distribution of possible rating changes and a slightly increased probability of stringing together enough positive results to hit 2521. Given she's occasionally achieved a 2500+ rating, reaching 2521 is difficult but not insurmountable for her over 6 months, justifying a gradual climb in probabilities to 0.11 in October 2025.
